panels.description: Status returned from the `/readyz` health status endpoint. Unhealthy reasons include beacon node problems, charon p2p problems, or validator client problems.
panels.title: Health Status - Peer

panels.description: Successful cluster duty percentage by type over the whole time range
panels.title: Cluster Duty Success(%)

panels.description:
Average beacon node score for the whole time window. The score is a combination of:
 - **errors**: the ratio or errors returned by the beacon node 
 - **latency**: how fast or slow the beacon node is responding


The performance of the Beacon node is critical to the performance of the validators and therefore rewards.

targets.expr:
(clamp(app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 1 OR app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 4 OR app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 5 OR app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 6, 1, 1) OR on() vector(0))
*
(
    (
0.5 * (1.0 - 10*(sum(increase(app_eth2_errors_total{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"}[$__range])) / (sum(increase(app_eth2_latency_seconds_count{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer",  endpoint !="submit_validator_registrations"}[$__range]))))) OR on() vector(0.5)
    )
    +
    (
        0.5 * (1.0 - clamp_max(histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(app_eth2_latency_seconds_bucket{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", endpoint !="submit_validator_registrations"}[$__range])) by (le)),1))
    )
)

panels.title: Beacon Node Score

panels.description:
Information about the charon cluster.

- **Network**: The blockchain network 
- **Operators/Nodes**: The number of operators or nodes or peers in the charon DV cluster.
- **Threshold**: The minimum number of participating nodes (also quorum).
- **Validators**: The number of validators added to the cluster, each validator is staking 32 ETH.

targets.expr:
( 1 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="mainnet"} )
or 
( 2 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="goerli"} )
or
( 3 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="sepolia"} )
or
( 4 * app_version{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", cluster_network="gnosis"} )

panels.title: Cluster Info

panels.description: Health checks that have failed at least once in the selected time window, based on the app_health_checks_failed_total counter.
panels.title: Health Checks History

panels.description: Status returned from the `/readyz` health status endpoint. Unhealthy reasons include beacon node problems, charon p2p problems, or validator client problems.
panels.title: Historical Health Status

panels.description:
- **Uptime**: Total time since cluster start time
- **Slot**: Current slot
- **Epoch**: Current Epoch
- **Validators Active**: Number of active validators in the cluster
- **Charon Peers Connected**: Number of Peers in the cluster
- **Relays Connected**: Number of Relays connected to the cluster
- **Beacon Node Peers**: Beacon Node Peers connected, based on app_beacon_node_peers
- **P2P Reachability**" Latest P2P reachability status, based on p2p_reachability_status
panels.title: Current Status

panels.description:
Status and balance of each validator by public key with links to beaconcha.in.

If present, the "❗️Peer Exits" column indicate the number of peers that submitted partially signed exits for each validator.
panels.title: Validators

panels.description:
Warning (w) and error (e) logs grouped by topic. 

Occasional warns and errors are normal, but a significant increase indicate problems that require operators to investigate by looking at the actual logs entries.
panels.title: Charon Warning(w) and Error(e) logs (per $interval)

panels.description: What percentage of Blocks are seen after the attestation kickoff time of 4 seconds. Validators that attest with data from this CL at that mark, before it is finished processing a valid block, are likely to see reduced rewards for an inaccurate head vote attestation as a result. Please seek to optimise your beacon node performance if this number is high.

targets.expr:
sort_desc(100 * (
  1 - 
  sum by (cluster_peer, nickname) (rate(app_beacon_node_sse_head_delay_bucket{cluster_name="$cluster_name", cluster_hash="$cluster_hash", le=~"4|4.0"}[$__range])) 
  / ignoring(le) 
  sum by (cluster_peer, nickname) (rate(app_beacon_node_sse_head_delay_bucket{cluster_name="$cluster_name", cluster_hash="$cluster_hash", le="+Inf"}[$__range]))
))

panels.title: % of Blocks Processed after 4s

panels.description: Any peer which is running on its secondary beacon nodes rather than its primary will be marked true here.
panels.title: Using Fallback Beacon Node?

names: {__name__="app_feature_flags", charon_version="v1.7.0-dev", cluster_hash="e0fa07c", cluster_name="test", cluster_network="testnet", cluster_peer="jittery-metal", feature_flags="fetch_only_commidx_0", instance="node0:3620", job="charon-0"}

panels.description: Completed duties by type over time.
panels.title: ✅ Completed duties by type

panels.description: Successful duty participation per peer (filtered by selected duty) over time.
panels.title: ✅ Successful Peer Participation: Duty=$duty

panels.description:
Failed duties by type over time. Use data link to show logs.
Proposer duties are non-failing after being successfully included on-chain.
Other broadcasted to the beacon node duties (i.e.: attester), are non-failing after being successfully broadcasted.
Any other duties not broadcasted to the beacon node (i.e.: randao), are non-failing after consensus is reached.
panels.title: ❌ Failed duties by type (per $interval)

panels.description: Missed duty participation per peer (filtered by selected duty) over time.
panels.title: 🚧 Missed Peer Participation: Duty=$duty

panels.description: Failed duties percentage by type over time
panels.title: ❌ Failed duty percentage by type (per $interval)

panels.description: Missed duty participation per duty (filtered by peer) over time.
panels.title: 🚧 Missed Peer Participation: Peer=$peer

panels.description: Number of failed duties by reason per time window. Note this is only available from v0.16.
panels.title: 🚨 Duty failure reason: $duty (per $interval)

panels.description: Duties that was successfully broadcast to the Beacon Node, but that was never included on-chain. This could be due to broadcasting too late (too slow), but it could also just be to aspects of consensus layer out-of-our-control. See https://eth2book.info/bellatrix/part2/incentives/rewards/#remarks.
panels.title: 💥 Broadcasted duties never included on-chain (per $interval)

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.90, sum(rate(core_parsigdb_store_bucket{cluster_name="$cluster_name", cluster_hash="$cluster_hash", cluster_peer="$cluster_peer", duty=~"$duty"}[$interval])) by (le, peer_idx))

panels.title: Signature received from peer since expected time: Duty=$duty

panels.description:
When a signature of attestation from a peer or own VC was registered at the parsigdb, compared to minimum expected time.

Minimum expected time for attestation signature is 4s after slot start.
panels.title: Signature received from peer since expected time: Duty=attester

panels.description:
When a signature of attestation from a peer or own VC was registered at the parsigdb, compared to minimum expected time.

Minimum expected time for attestation signature is 4s after slot start.
panels.title: Signature received from peer since expected time: Duty=proposer

panels.description:
The delay since the expected duty submission time.
For duties broadcasted to the beacon node (i.e.: proposer), successful beacon node response is taken as completion time.
For duties not broadcasted to the beacon node (i.e.: randao), completed consensus is taken as completion time.
panels.title: Submission delay: $duty

panels.description: The leader of final consensus round represented as its cluster peer index. This is the node who proposed the value the cluster came to consensus on to sign.
panels.title: Node that chose the proposal data

panels.description: The leader of final consensus round represented as its cluster peer index. This is the node who proposed the value the cluster came to consensus on to sign.
panels.title: Node that chose the attester data

panels.description: How long in total did it take to come to consensus on this/these duties? Duties generally have a 4 second window to be completed (1.25s on gnosis chain), so consensus should not take more than half of that time for good performance.
panels.title: Consensus duration: $duty

panels.description: The number of consensus rounds it takes to achieve consensus on a batch of duties. When every node is online, this should be 1 round, if it is taking more rounds than the fault tolerance of the cluster to come to consensus, there may be an issue with node operators in the cluster, or the latency of the cluster.
panels.title: Consensus Rounds: $duty

panels.description: Number of consensus timeouts per time window.
panels.title: 🚨 Consensus Timeouts (per $interval)

panels.description: Total time spent on garbage collection per interval
panels.title: Garbage Collection Duration (per $interval)

panels.description: Beacon node score over time

targets.expr:
clamp_min((clamp(app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 1 OR app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 4 OR app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 5 OR app_monitoring_readyz{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"} == 6, 1, 1) OR on() vector(0))
*
(
    (
0.5 * (1.0 - 10*(sum(increase(app_eth2_errors_total{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"}[$interval])) / (sum(increase(app_eth2_latency_seconds_count{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"}[$interval]))))) OR on() vector(0.5)
    )
    +
    (
        0.5 * (1.0 - clamp_max(histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(app_eth2_latency_seconds_bucket{cluster_name="$cluster_name",cluster_hash="$cluster_hash",cluster_peer="$cluster_peer"}[$interval])) by (le)),1))
    )
), 0)

panels.title: Beacon Node Score

panels.description: Number of consensus layer P2P peers the beacon node is connected to
panels.title: Beacon node peer count

panels.description: Upstream beacon node API request rate per second by endpoint
panels.title: Beacon Node API requests rate

panels.description: Upstream beacon node API request latency by endpoint by timewindow
panels.title: Beacon Node API request latency

panels.description: Number of errors per endpoint per time window
panels.title: 🚨 Beacon Node API error count (per $interval)

panels.description: Upstream beacon node API proxied request latency by endpoint by timewindow
panels.title: Beacon Node API proxied request latency

panels.description: An overview of when in the slot a block is finished processing by the connected CL.  Blocks processed after 4 seconds (i.e. in the 4.5 bar and higher) miss the attestation kick-off, so if your cluster is attesting to this slot, and this node is the leader, it will propose an attestation to an incorrect head of the chain, and result in reduced rewards.
panels.title: When in a slot a block finishes processing

panels.description: Time in seconds between block gossip and head events, indicating block processing time, averaged across all connected beacon nodes.
panels.title: Block Processing Time

panels.description: Charon caches certain calls to the beacon node. The bigger the amount of cache hits seen in this panel, the more requests Charon saved to the BN and improved its health.
panels.title: Cache hits total

panels.description: Charon caches certain calls to the beacon node. The lower the number of misses the better. It is expected to have 1-2 misses every epoch.
panels.title: Cache misses total

panels.description: Charon caches certain calls to the beacon node. On reorg event from the beacon node, Charon invalidates its cache.
panels.title: Cache invalidation due reorg

panels.description: Whether this node has a growing number of relay registration failures, indicating there may be a risk to an upcoming MEV block production opportunity. No data may mean MEV registration is working correctly, or that it is not enabled at all.
panels.title: MEV Relay Submit Registrations Failure Growth

panels.description: The fetch proposal from peer was locally built or built by MEV (blinded).
panels.title: Blinded or Local block received

panels.description: Number of errors per endpoint per time window
panels.title: 🚨 Validator API error count (per $interval)

panels.description:
Type of connections to each peer in the cluster during the time window:
 - 🟢 Green: Direct connection (this is the preferred)
 - 🟡 Yellow: Indirect relay connection (this is ok if only temporary)
 - 🔴 Red: No connection to peer (this is a problem)

See https://docs.obol.tech/docs/charon/networking#external-p2p-network
panels.title: Peer Connections (🟢=direct, 🟡=relay, 🔴=not connected)

panels.description: Number of new libp2p connections per peer per time window
panels.title: New P2P Connections (per $interval)

panels.description: Time windows that contain ping errors per peer
panels.title: Ping Errors

panels.description: Avg Rate of P2P network bytes sent plus received per peer (bytes/second)
panels.title: P2P Network Avg Rate Rx+Tx

panels.description: Total P2P network bytes sent plus received per peer per time window (bytes per interval)
panels.title: P2P Network Bytes Rx+Tx (per $interval)

panels.description: An overview of when in the slot a block is finished processing by the connected CL.  Blocks processed after 4 seconds (i.e. in the 4.5 bar and higher) miss the attestation kick-off, so if your cluster is attesting to this slot, and this node is the leader, it will propose an attestation to an incorrect head of the chain, and result in reduced rewards.
panels.title: Avg Handshake duration by Transport

panels.description: The number of active libp2p streams by peer or relay. Note this can be further grouped by direction and protocol.
panels.title: Active libp2p streams
